content.verdict: Sandals Royal Plantation is the most refined and intimate of the Sandals portfolio — 74 suites only, all-butler-service, on a private bluff between two private beach coves on the Ocho Rios coast. Originally Plantation Inn, a celebrity hideaway in the 1950s where Bing Crosby and Frank Sinatra stayed, it was acquired and rebuilt by Sandals as their flagship boutique adults-only product. For honeymoon couples who want Sandals' all-inclusive convenience but without the mass-market scale of the larger Sandals resorts, this is the right Sandals choice in Jamaica.
content.best_room: All 74 rooms at Royal Plantation are suites with butler service — the entry-level Honeymoon Hideaway Oceanfront Suites already include a private balcony with ocean views and dedicated butler. The premium Plantation Royale Suites are the original 1950s Plantation Inn rooms reimagined, with separate sitting rooms and the largest balconies on the bluff. The single Royal Penthouse is a two-bedroom apartment-style suite with a rooftop hot tub.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival & Butler Welcome
itinerary_7_nights.description: Fly into Sangster (MBJ) and transfer 90 minutes east to Ocho Rios — the included Club Sandals lounge service makes the airport transfer easy (Ian Fleming OCJ is 20 minutes if connections allow). Butler-served arrival on the suite balcony, all-inclusive wristband, and a first dinner at Le Papillon, the formal French restaurant on property.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Twin Beach Day & Scuba Intro
itinerary_7_nights.description: Royal Plantation's two private beach coves are on either side of the bluff — connected by the cliff-top pool deck. Morning at the more sheltered east cove for swimming, afternoon at the surf-side west cove. The included scuba program runs daily for certified divers; non-divers can do the resort intro course at no extra cost.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Dunn's River Falls
itinerary_7_nights.description: Dunn's River Falls is just 10 minutes west — included Sandals shuttle excursion goes early to beat the cruise crowds. Climbing the 180-foot terraced cascade is the iconic Jamaica photo. Lunch back at the resort, afternoon at the Red Lane Spa for a couples' massage in a beach cabana.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Mystic Mountain & Bobsled
itinerary_7_nights.description: Mystic Mountain is a 15-minute drive — the chairlift up the rainforest canopy and the bobsled-track ride down (Jamaica's bobsled team trains here) is genuinely fun and very Jamaica. The included Sandals shuttle runs morning trips. Return to the resort for the included afternoon sushi at Kimonos.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Bob Marley Mausoleum
itinerary_7_nights.description: Excursion 90 minutes inland to Nine Mile in St. Ann's Parish — Bob Marley's birthplace and burial site. The Marley family-run tour of the simple wooden house, the rock pillow he slept on, and the marble mausoleum painted in Rasta colours is a moving Jamaica highlight. Return via a Blue Mountain coffee plantation tasting.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Catamaran Cruise & GoldenEye Visit
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning private boat charter (or included group catamaran) east along the coast past GoldenEye and Firefly — the option to dock for a tour of Ian Fleming's Bond writing house adds genuine cultural depth. Afternoon at the Royal Plantation pool deck above the bluff, then a final formal dinner at Le Papillon.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Final Beach Morning & Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Butler-served breakfast on the suite balcony — fresh fruit, ackee and saltfish, and Blue Mountain coffee — last swim in the sheltered cove, and the included transfer back to MBJ. The Sandals returning-guest programme tracks repeat honeymoons and rewards anniversary returns.

caveats: Royal Plantation's bluff setting means beach access is via stairs down to the coves — beautiful but not step-free; couples with mobility considerations should request specific suites near elevators.
The 'beaches' are two small private coves rather than a long beach — perfect for swimming and snorkeling but not for long sand walks; plan a Negril or Seven Mile day trip for that.
The Sandals all-inclusive food across 6 restaurants is good but not on Caribbean fine-dining benchmarks — Le Papillon is the strongest, Kimonos sushi is solid, the buffet is typical.

faqs.question: Good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Royal Plantation is the most honeymoon-appropriate Sandals in Jamaica — all-suite, all-butler, only 74 rooms, adults-only, and an unusually refined atmosphere compared to Sandals' larger resorts. The Honeymoon Romance package adds welcome champagne, in-suite breakfast, candlelit dinner, and a couples' spa credit. Sandals' return-guest programme means many honeymooners come back for anniversaries.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: Mid-December through April is peak — sunny, dry, calm seas. May and June offer excellent value before summer humidity. August through October has hurricane risk. November is excellent shoulder. The Sandals weddings and honeymoon programme runs strongest in spring and fall.

faqs.question: Book in advance?
faqs.answer: Honeymoon Hideaway suites book 4 to 6 months ahead for high season. The Royal Penthouse and Plantation Royale Suites need 6 to 9 months. Sandals often runs 'free wedding' packages for honeymoon stays of 6+ nights, which can substantially change the value calculation.

faqs.question: Adults-only?
faqs.answer: Yes — all Sandals resorts (including Royal Plantation) are strictly adults-only (18+). The smaller scale of Royal Plantation (74 suites vs. 500+ at larger Sandals) makes it more genuinely couples-focused than the bigger properties in the chain.

faqs.question: Best room type?
faqs.answer: The Plantation Royale Suites in the original 1950s wing have the most character — separate sitting rooms, largest balconies, and the property's best ocean views. Honeymoon Hideaway Oceanfront Suites are the strongest entry-level value with butler service included. The Royal Penthouse is the splurge.

faqs.question: How to get there?
faqs.answer: Sangster International (MBJ) in Montego Bay is the main gateway — 90-minute private Sandals transfer east to Ocho Rios (included for Club Sandals categories). Ian Fleming International (OCJ) at Boscobel is just 20 minutes away if regional connections work.
